IRS regulations require that we issue 1099 forms to certain companies and individuals. In order to accurately prepare these forms, IRS requires that we obtain and maintain form W-9 (or substitute form W-9) for all of our vendors. Therefore, in order to ensure our reporting accuracy, please complete the enclosed substitute form W-9 and return to us preferably by email, fax, or mail.
Thank you for your immediate attention to this matter.
Sincerely,

Dear Purchasing Manager,

I want to take this opportunity to introduce Asia Engineered Components & Assembly Co., Ltd. (AECA). AECA offers casting, forging & solid stock with High- Precision Machining (3 & 5 axis CNC Turning/Milling), and fabrication. AECA is your full service manufacturing partner.

It is our intention to provide needed services to our customers.

1. Inspection - process control, functional test, reliability test & PPAP III documents available.
2. In house making to reduce lead time and consolidate components which will create added value & shipment to market demand.
3. Competitive pricing and minimal tooling cost.
4. QC process improvement services:
• Special process validation
• Capability Studies
• SPC Data
• Value engineering to reduce manufacturing cost
• Inventory Management (KANBAN sizing, JIT Shipments).
• Gage R & R
• ISO 9001: 2008

AECA is supplying high quality parts or assemblies, on time delivery (OTD) and offers competitive prices for customers . We have big company capabilities with small company sensibilities.

Please send us drawings or samples for quotation.

Thanks
Best regards,

- Weight empty per Sq./Mtr.

- Internal lining material
- Insulation material
- Insulation thickness

- Standard Internal dimensions (in mm)
- Material fixing type (if used)
- Cold life without opening
- Thermal Conductance (W/m².K)

- Life span of the product (optimal condition)
- Any other technical information
- Unit cost of the product for 100 pcs

Table.Details: To cover frozen or refrigerated food for transport to Food Bank

We're looking for a solution to our current dilemma: we manufacture probiotic supplements that need to maintain a temperature of 45F or below throughout transit from Phoenix to Houston. We currently ship on a standard GMA pallet and the height is typically less than 48" tall. Transit time is 2 days.

We're interested in learning more about the capabilities of the pallet covers/blankets as well as the insulated boxes.
Table.Comments: We ship LTL freight for most shipments; there is only one refrigerated truck route from Phoenix to Houston that we are able to utilize. It is not very cost effective and tracking is not available. If there is a solution to maintain an average temp of 45F or less and still ship LTL, then you'll have our business instantly!

Table.ProductsInterestedIn: Return Air Bulkheads, Chutes and Adapters
Table.Details:
We are purchasing a new Utility 53' reefer equipped w/ a Carrier 7500x4 refrigeration unit. We have an interior height concern. Trailer interior height is specified to be 105.875". Product to be transported is extremely bulky --- 103" tall as loaded and requires transport @ < 0 degrees F.
We are looking for the thinnest and shortest transition adaptor available from the unit to the chute together w/ a recommendation as to chute type to move the greatest volume of air to rear of trailer. Preferably, the transition adaptor height at air outlet would not exceed 2.5", however width could be as needed up to 98" if necessary. Perhaps it could even have a divided plenum and use dual 48" wide chutes if necessary for air volume. Basically, we need to get the refrigerated air from the unit discharge to the roof of the trailer as quickly as possible w/o losing velocity or volume and then get it to the rear of the trailer. Any suggestions?

Table.Comments: we are looking for price on the vinyl curtains for a reefer trailer if you could also include details thanks

Table.Details: i got an 07 wasbash refer trailer

Table.Comments:
Hello!

Please allow me to take a minute of your time to introduce myself. My name is Meagan Erne, and I am part of the show management team that is producing the brand-new World of Transportation Expo @ the Greater Columbus Convention Center, October 29th-31st 2015! I am writing today to give you a little more insight into this show, in hopes of gaining your participation as an exhibitor (or attendee) within this show!


The World of Transportation Expo (WTE) is Ohio’s premier trade show and encompasses “the entire world” within the trucking and transportation industry. Throughout this 3-day event, world class exhibitors will showcase their newest products and technology to our national audience of attendees. WTE brings together major truck, trailer, engine and truck body manufacture dealers, along with numerous parts and service suppliers, industry publications, organizations and recruiters, just to name a few!

Taking place at the Greater Columbus Convention Center, the “true epicenter” of the transportation industry, World of Transportation Expo will spotlight three major segments within the industry:
~All Things “Trucks, Tanks & Trailers”: Dealers, manufacturers, parts suppliers, engines, fleet software, GPS, transportation insurance, safety equipment, towing, cleaning equipment, fuels/additives, etc.
~Energy Systems and Environmental Responsibility: Alternative fuels, CNG & LNG, natural gas, sustainability, to name a few!
~Recruitment and Education: A spotlighted Recruitment center, trade schools, owner-operators, industry associations and organizations; Educational Seminars organized in partnership with The Ohio Trucking Association and Clean Fuels Ohio.

We would love for you to join this new show as an exhibitor. Coinciding with our OTA partnership, we are offering a 10% booth space discount to current & new OTA members. In addition, we have a variety of sponsorship, advertising and promotional opportunities available as well! We are also accepting proposals to host education seminars, breakout-sessions, social events, etc.

Please feel free to contact me directly at any time with any questions that you may have! Booth sales are currently open, and filling up! Please visit our website @ www.worldoftransportationexpo.com to see more details about the show! I will soon follow-up with a phone call as well! Thank you very much for your time and consideration! Looking forward to hearing back from you!

Sincerely,
Meagan A. Erne

To View the most recent update from WTE: http://conta.cc/1MP86SI
